# cloudflare_r2_data_catalog (Data Source)



## Example Usage

```terraform
data "cloudflare_r2_data_catalog" "example_r2_data_catalog" {
  account_id = "0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef"
  bucket_name = "my-data-bucket"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `account_id` (String) Use this to identify the account.
- `bucket_name` (String) Specifies the R2 bucket name.

### Read-Only

- `bucket` (String) Specifies the associated R2 bucket name.
- `credential_status` (String) Shows the credential configuration status.
Available values: "present", "absent".
- `id` (String) Specifies the R2 bucket name.
- `maintenance_config` (Attributes) Configures maintenance for the catalog. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--maintenance_config))
- `name` (String) Specifies the catalog name (generated from account and bucket name).
- `status` (String) Indicates the status of the catalog.
Available values: "active", "inactive".

<a id="nestedatt--maintenance_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance_config`

Read-Only:

- `compaction` (Attributes) Configures compaction for catalog maintenance. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--maintenance_config--compaction))
- `snapshot_expiration` (Attributes) Configures snapshot expiration settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--maintenance_config--snapshot_expiration))

<a id="nestedatt--maintenance_config--compaction"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance_config.compaction`

Read-Only:

- `state` (String) Specifies the state of maintenance operations.
Available values: "enabled", "disabled".
- `target_size_mb` (String) Sets the target file size for compaction in megabytes. Defaults to "128".
Available values: "64", "128", "256", "512".


<a id="nestedatt--maintenance_config--snapshot_expiration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance_config.snapshot_expiration`

Read-Only:

- `max_snapshot_age` (String) Specifies the maximum age for snapshots. The system deletes snapshots older than this age.
Format: <number><unit> where unit is d (days), h (hours), m (minutes), or s (seconds).
Examples: "7d" (7 days), "48h" (48 hours), "2880m" (2,880 minutes).
Defaults to "7d".
- `min_snapshots_to_keep` (Number) Specifies the minimum number of snapshots to retain. Defaults to 100.
- `state` (String) Specifies the state of maintenance operations.
Available values: "enabled", "disabled".
